title: abies recurvata subsp. ernestii
intro: Abies recurvata subsp. ernestii is a subspecies of the Abies recurvata tree, commonly known as the Guanacaste fir, native to China and specifically found in the mountainous regions of Yunnan and Sichuan provinces. The bark and leaves of the plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Abies recurvata subsp. ernestii include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties and to promote overall well-being.
